https://www.nxcode.io/resources/news/gro.title: Grok 5 Release Date: Latest News & What We Know (April 2026)
https://www.nxcode.io/resources/news/gro.text:
URL Source: https://www.nxcode.io/resources/news/grok-5-release-date-latest-news-2026

Published Time: 2026-03-24T00:00:00.000Z

Markdown Content:
## The Latest on Grok 5 (March 2026 Update)

Grok 5 has not been released as of March 2026. [Elon Musk originally confirmed](https://phemex.com/news/article/elon-musk-confirms-grok-5-launch-for-q1-2026-36075) a Q1 2026 launch, but that window has passed. xAI's official X account now points to **Q2 2026** as the most likely release window. The model carries a reported [6 trillion parameters](https://news.aibase.com/news/22865) using a Mixture-of-Experts architecture and is training on xAI's [1-gigawatt Colossus 2 supercluster](https://www.teslarati.com/elon-musk-xai-brings-1gw-colossus-2-ai-training-cluster-online/) in Memphis, Tennessee. For context, its predecessor [Grok 4 scored 88% on GPQA Diamond](https://x.ai/news/grok-4) and 25.4% on Humanity's Last Exam.

Here is what has happened in the last 30 days:

*   **March 3, 2026** -- xAI shipped Grok 4.20 Beta 2 with five targeted fixes: better instruction following, fewer hallucinations, enhanced LaTeX support, more accurate image search, and improved multi-image rendering. This is the current publicly available model, not Grok 5.
*   **February 25, 2026** -- Grok's official X account updated its projection, pointing to **Q2 2026** as the most likely window for Grok 5's full release.
*   **February 17-18, 2026** -- Grok 4.20 Beta launched with a 4-agent system, followed immediately by Grok 4.20 Heavy with 16 specialized agents. These multi-agent architectures are widely viewed as stepping stones toward Grok 5.
*   **January 2026** -- xAI confirmed Colossus 2, its 1-gigawatt supercluster in Memphis, Tennessee, is fully operational and actively training Grok 5.

The bottom line: Grok 5 missed its Q1 deadline. The new consensus points to Q2 2026.

* * *

## Expected Timeline: When Is Grok 5 Coming Out?

Based on all available signals, here is the most likely timeline:

| Milestone | Expected Window |
| --- | --- |
| Training completion | April 2026 |
| Internal testing / red-teaming | April-May 2026 |
| Public beta (X Premium+) | May-June 2026 |
| Full API access | Q3 2026 |

**Why Q2?** Three key factors point to this window:

1.   **Colossus 2 upgrade to 1.5GW in April**: Musk confirmed that the supercluster is expanding from 1GW to 1.5GW by April 2026. This upgrade aligns with the completion of Grok 5's primary training run -- the additional capacity would support fine-tuning and inference at scale.

2.   **Polymarket odds**: The prediction market gives a 33% probability that Grok 5 ships by June 30, 2026, and only 1% by March 31. The smart money says Q2.

https://thezvi.substack.com/p/gpt-54-is-.title: GPT-5.4 Is A Substantial Upgrade
https://thezvi.substack.com/p/gpt-54-is-.text:
URL Source: https://thezvi.substack.com/p/gpt-54-is-a-substantial-upgrade

Published Time: 2026-03-11T14:00:13+00:00

Markdown Content:
Benchmarks have never been less useful for telling us which models are best.

They are good for giving a general sense of the landscape. They definitely paint a picture. But if you’re comparing top models, like GPT-5.4 against Opus 4.6 against Gemini 3.1 Pro, you have to use the models, talk to the models, get reports from those who have and form a gestalt. The reports will contradict each other and you have to work through that. There’s no other way.

Thus, I try to gather and sort a reasonably comprehensive set of reactions, so you can browse the sections that make you most curious.

The gestalt is that GPT-5.4 is a very good model, sir. It’s a substantial upgrade from GPT-5.2, and also from 5.3-Codex, and it puts OpenAI back in the game, whereas I felt like Opus 4.6 dominated OpenAI’s previous offerings for all but narrow uses.

Each lab’s models vary and things change over time, but they tend to have consistent strengths, weaknesses and personalities. From what I’ve seen this is very much an OpenAI model. It’s highly capable, and it is especially seen as a big improvement by the whisperers and those who watch LLMs interact with each other, but it’s not aspiring to be a Claude.

GPT-5.4 Self-Portrait

GPT-5.4 seems like a substantial upgrade over GPT-5.2.

GPT-5.4 seems excellent so far at assembling facts and giving your the rundown, or figuring out what is happening, and other things like that.

I haven’t coded anything since GPT-5.4 came out. It’s clearly good at coding. One key question people are split on is whether it is good at solving for your intent.

Many are reporting that its writing and personality are much improved, and that it can now be used for writing and editing in spots previous models were not useful.

They are claiming strong computer use but no one seems to be testing that either way.

It costs more than GPT-5.2 per token. In some places it gets that back in efficiency, but [overall AA reports](https://artificialanalysis.ai/) costs modestly rose from $2304 to $2951. Opus is more expensive ($4970) in max mode, but cheaper ($1451) in normal mode. GPT-5.4-Pro is of course by far the most expensive thing out there, so if you want it then lean on that subscription.

GPT-5.4 is not a step change in core general capabilities. The preparedness framework scores make this clear, and there are various signs that OpenAI’s strategy is focusing on hitting internal metrics and improving the most common use cases. In practice that can be highly useful.

The ‘model relations department,’ those concerned with multi-model interactions and model welfare and consciousness and so on, see this as a big step forward for OpenAI. There’s still a long way to go.

I haven’t noticed much personality from it, and I get more joy from Claude Opus 4.6 than I do from GPT-5.4, but I don’t ask those questions so much.

https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse.title: MLQ.ai | AI for investors
https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse.text:
URL Source: https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/

Markdown Content:
Meta unveiled Muse Spark on April 8, 2026, its first AI model from the newly formed Meta Superintelligence Labs. The natively multimodal model handles text, images, audio, and tools, competing with leading systems from OpenAI and Google on benchmarks like GPQA and HealthBench.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[2](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[3](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")

## Model Capabilities and Reasoning Modes

Muse Spark features three reasoning modes: Instant for quick responses, Thinking for chain-of-thought on harder problems, and Contemplating, which deploys multiple agents in parallel to solve complex tasks faster than sequential methods. It supports visual chain-of-thought, allowing step-by-step reasoning on images, and multi-agent orchestration for tasks like trip planning with subagents handling itineraries and activities simultaneously.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[2](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source") The model was trained with reinforcement learning that penalizes excessive thinking time, enabling efficient performance on math and science problems.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")

## Benchmark Performance and Health Focus

On HealthBench Hard, Muse Spark achieved 42.8, surpassing GPT-5.4 at 40.1 and Gemini 3.1 Pro at 20.6, with training data curated by over 1,000 physicians for health reasoning like nutrition and drug information.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[4](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source") It excels in visual STEM tasks, generating interactive displays and minigames, and performs strongly in multimodal reasoning under independent evaluation.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[3](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")

## Deployment and Infrastructure Overhaul

Markdown Content:
# Anthropic Just Cut Off Claude Subscriptions from OpenClaw | TryOpenClaw.ai
[![Image 1: OpenClaw logo](https://www.tryopenclaw.ai/icon.svg) TryOpenClaw](https://www.tryopenclaw.ai/)

[Try for $1 →](https://www.tryopenclaw.ai/signup/?v=t1_m39&src=header)

# Anthropic just cut off Claude subscriptions from OpenClaw

By [Linas Valiukas](https://valiukas.dev/) · April 4, 2026

Anthropic sent an email this morning. Starting today — April 4, 12pm PT — your Claude subscription no longer covers OpenClaw. Want to keep using it? Turn on "extra usage," a pay-as-you-go layer billed on top of your existing subscription.

This isn't a surprise if you've been following the story. Anthropic [quietly blocked subscription OAuth tokens back in January](https://www.tryopenclaw.ai/blog/anthropic-google-banning-openclaw-users/). But that was silent, technical, deniable. This is an email to every subscriber, with a date and a deadline. They're making it official.

## What the email says

The email, [shared on Hacker News](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47633396) by user firloop, lays it out plainly:

> "Starting April 4 at 12pm PT / 8pm BST, you'll no longer be able to use your Claude subscription limits for third-party harnesses including OpenClaw. You can still use them with your Claude account, but they will require extra usage, a pay-as-you-go option billed separately from your subscription."

Your subscription still covers Claude Code and Claude Cowork. Anything else now needs separate billing. They're starting enforcement with OpenClaw today and say more third-party tools will follow.

The sweeteners: a one-time credit equal to your monthly plan price ($20 for Pro, $100 for Max 5x, $200 for Max 20x), redeemable by April 17. Discounts up to 30% on pre-purchased token bundles. And a refund option coming in a follow-up email.

Although some people on Reddit [are reporting](https://www.reddit.com/r/Anthropic/comments/1sbsfct/anthropic_bans_using_claude_subscription_for_3rd/) that the credit redemption doesn't actually work. So there's that.

## The justification — and the real reason

Boris Cherny, Anthropic's Head of Claude Code, [told VentureBeat](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-cuts-off-the-ability-to-use-claude-subscriptions-with-openclaw-and): "We've been working hard to meet the increase in demand for Claude, and our subscriptions weren't built for the usage patterns of these third-party tools." He added that "capacity is a resource we manage thoughtfully and we are prioritizing our customers using our products and API."

# NVIDIA NIM API Explained: Free AI Inference in 2026

2026-04-01 by [Ignacy](https://decodethefuture.org/en/author/dekorart600gmail-com/ "View all posts by Ignacy")

Last updated: April 2026 · Tested by the author on [build.nvidia.com](https://build.nvidia.com/)

NVIDIA NIM (NVIDIA Inference Microservices) is a platform that gives developers free, OpenAI-compatible API access to over 100 AI models — including Nemotron, Kimi-K2.5, MiniMax-M2.5, and GLM-5 — hosted on DGX Cloud at [build.nvidia.com](https://build.nvidia.com/). Beyond API endpoints, the platform offers GPU sandbox instances on Blackwell and Hopper hardware (up to 288 GiB VRAM on B300), pre-built Blueprints for agentic workflows, and the newly launched NemoClaw security stack for safe autonomous agent execution. Developers get 1,000 free inference credits on signup with a rate limit of 40 requests per minute — enough for meaningful prototyping before committing to self-hosted deployment.

NVIDIA NIM API Free Inference NemoClaw Blackwell GPU Developer Tools

# Nvidia-supported SiFive reaches a $3.65 billion valuation with its open AI chip technology

[101 finance](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/11/nvidia-backed-sifive-hits-3-65-billion-valuation-for-open-ai-chips/)2026/04/11 14:12

By: 101 finance

## SiFive Secures $400 Million in Funding, Reaching $3.65 Billion Valuation

SiFive, established in 2015 by engineers from UC Berkeley who pioneered an open-source chip architecture, has successfully raised an impressive $400 million in an oversubscribed funding round, bringing the company’s valuation to $3.65 billion.

### Why This Investment Matters

This development is notable for several reasons. Unlike the dominant x86 and ARM processors that power most of today’s AI hardware, SiFive’s RISC-V architecture offers an open alternative. These two established CPU types are the backbone of Nvidia’s AI computing systems, making SiFive’s approach a significant departure from the norm.

### Major Backers and Investors

Nvidia participated in this funding round, joining a diverse group of venture capitalists, private equity firms, and hedge funds. The round was led by Atreides Management, founded by former Fidelity executive Gavin Baker, who also invested in Cerebras Systems’ $1 billion round. Other notable investors include Apollo Global Management, D1 Capital Partners, Point72 Turion, T. Rowe Price, Sutter Hill Ventures, and several others.

### SiFive’s Licensing Model

SiFive operates similarly to how Arm once did, by licensing its chip blueprints to companies who then adapt them for their own products, rather than manufacturing chips themselves. In contrast, Arm shifted its strategy in March by launching its first in-house AI chip, developed in partnership with Meta and adopted by clients such as OpenAI, Cerebras, and Cloudflare.

### Open and Independent Chip Design

SiFive distinguishes itself with open, non-proprietary chip designs that remain vendor-neutral and aren’t tied to specific customers. According to Pitchbook, the company’s last fundraising was in March 2022, when it secured $175 million led by Coatue Management at a pre-money valuation of $2.33 billion. That round also included Intel Capital, Qualcomm Ventures, and Aramco Ventures.

# Microsoft starts removing unnecessary Copilot buttons in Windows 11

## Notepad's Copilot menu has been replaced with 'writing tools.'

[Mariella Moon](https://www.engadget.com/about/editors/mariella-moon/)

Contributing Reporter

Fri, April 10, 2026 at 12:03 PM UTC

[Add Engadget on Google](https://www.google.com/preferences/source?q=engadget.com)

Fri, April 10, 2026 at 12:03 PM UTC

![Image 2](https://s.yimg.com/ny/api/res/1.2/7EDT9kHqQfd4ahqS1kl.vg--/YXBwaWQ9aGlnaGxhbmRlcjt3PTEyNDI7aD04Mjg-/https://s.yimg.com/uu/api/res/1.2/rMo0ShH0VZIH8_l9uCSfbg--~B/aD0yMzQ2O3c9MzUxOTthcHBpZD15dGFjaHlvbg--/https://d29szjachogqwa.cloudfront.net/images/2026-04/99dd69a0-cee2-43c7-aec6-29fc90a716e3)

Photo by Joan Cros/NurPhoto via Getty Images (NurPhoto via Getty Images)

[Microsoft](https://www.engadget.com/computing/microsoft-will-yank-copilot-from-some-windows-apps-and-let-you-move-the-taskbar-again-202857203.html) has rolled out a Notepad update for Windows Insiders that removes the Copilot branding and icon from within the app, [_Windows Central_](https://www.windowscentral.com/microsoft/windows-11/microsoft-finally-begins-removing-copilot-from-notepad-on-windows-11-but-the-ai-still-persists) has reported. The old Copilot menu has been replaced with “writing tools,” but it’s worth noting that the tools are still powered by AI and are pretty much identical to the selection found in the old menu. Microsoft has just replaced the Copilot button with a pen icon. In addition, the company has removed mentions of AI in the Settings menu and has placed the option to disable the AI-powered writing tools within the “Advanced features” section.

https://eu.36kr.com/en/p/376439198292455.text:
URL Source: https://eu.36kr.com/en/p/3764391982924552

Markdown Content:
# OpenAI Fears the New Models It Has Trained

English

中文

Deutsch

[](https://eu.36kr.com/en/p/3764391982924552)[](https://eu.36kr.com/zh/p/3764391982924552)[](https://eu.36kr.com/de/p/3764391982924552)

[Home](https://eu.36kr.com/en)Article

# OpenAI has also started to fear the new models it has trained.

[新智元](https://eu.36kr.com/en/p/3764391982924552)2026-04-13 09:07

Altman: You should also be afraid of my new model! 😨

> **Seeing the huge impact and high level of discussion brought about by the powerful cyber attack and defense capabilities of Anthropic's next - generation flagship model, Mythos, which is in internal testing, Sam Altman couldn't sit still and planned an internal test of OpenAI's AI with powerful cyber attack and defense capabilities.**

On April 9th, Axios reported that OpenAI is preparing a product with strong cybersecurity capabilities, which will only be initially available to a small number of partners.

![Image 1](https://img.36krcdn.com/hsossms/20260413/v2_d20741134cf849d99e52aeb91815f19a@5091053_oswg948336oswg1080oswg1105_img_000?x-oss-process=image/format,jpg/interlace,1)

https://x.com/axios/status/2042244222292529371

What's really worth discussing behind this is not what new thing OpenAI is about to release.

It's another scarier thing: AI may have truly crossed the line in the field of cybersecurity.

If you've been following AI in the past few months, you'll notice a particularly subtle change.

Two years ago, when people talked about models, they discussed whether they could write copy, make PPTs, or write code.

Later, the discussion shifted to agents, automatic execution, and whether they could call tools on their own.

Now, the discussion has veered in another direction.

Can it find vulnerabilities on its own, reproduce them, and exploit them?

These questions sound like topics for an internal meeting in the security circle, not something ordinary people would care about.

But frankly, once the answer starts to lean towards "yes", this is no longer just a small piece of news in the hacker community.

This is a major event at the infrastructure level.

Because a vulnerability is not as simple as restarting your computer after a blue - screen error.

Vulnerabilities are connected to water treatment plants, power grids, hospitals, banks, browsers, operating systems, and cloud services.

In the past, these vulnerabilities were mainly discovered slowly by top - level security researchers, red teams, and national - level institutions.

Now, models are getting involved.

And it's not just about helping you complete a couple of lines of code. It's more like you give it a task at night, and when you wake up the next morning, it has already laid out the PoC, the exploitation chain, and repair suggestions on your desk.

## **Just a simple imitation of Anthropic Mythos'**

## **PR strategy?**

Recently, Anthropic also took an unusual action.

https://mistral.ai/news/mistral-3.title: Introducing Mistral 3
https://mistral.ai/news/mistral-3.text:
URL Source: https://mistral.ai/news/mistral-3

Published Time: 2025-12-02T16:00:00

Markdown Content:
Today, we announce Mistral 3, the next generation of Mistral models. Mistral 3 includes three state-of-the-art small, dense models (14B, 8B, and 3B) and Mistral Large 3 – our most capable model to date – a sparse mixture-of-experts trained with 41B active and 675B total parameters. All models are released under the Apache 2.0 license. Open-sourcing our models in a variety of compressed formats empowers the developer community and puts AI in people’s hands through distributed intelligence.

The Ministral models represent the best performance-to-cost ratio in their category. At the same time, Mistral Large 3 joins the ranks of frontier instruction-fine-tuned open-source models.

## Mistral Large 3: A state-of-the-art open model

![Image 1: Chart Base Models (1)](https://cms.mistral.ai/assets/98aeee04-e1c3-43b7-b90e-c51da84d5e56.png?width=1905&height=1242)

![Image 2: 3 Model Performance Comparison (instruct)](https://cms.mistral.ai/assets/bdf27a12-76fd-4e62-be9b-938f14288a9a.png?width=1346&height=1115)

Mistral Large 3 is one of the best permissive open weight models in the world, trained from scratch on 3000 of NVIDIA’s H200 GPUs. Mistral Large 3 is Mistral’s first mixture-of-experts model since the seminal Mixtral series, and represents a substantial step forward in pretraining at Mistral. After post-training, the model achieves parity with the best instruction-tuned open-weight models on the market on general prompts, while also demonstrating image understanding and best-in-class performance on multilingual conversations (i.e., non-English/Chinese).

Mistral Large 3 debuts at #2 in the OSS non-reasoning models category (#6 amongst OSS models overall) on the [LMArena leaderboard](https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text).

![Image 3: Lm Arena Chart Ml3](https://cms.mistral.ai/assets/4626af3d-7554-4d50-9c0e-041fe7111ece.png?width=1905&height=1242)

We release both the base and instruction fine-tuned versions of Mistral Large 3 under the Apache 2.0 license, providing a strong foundation for further customization across the enterprise and developer communities. A reasoning version is coming soon!

### Mistral, NVIDIA, vLLM & Red Hat join forces to deliver faster, more accessible Mistral 3

Working in conjunction with vLLM and Red Hat, Mistral Large 3 is very accessible to the open-source community. We’re releasing a checkpoint in NVFP4 format, built with [llm-compressor](https://github.com/vllm-project/llm-compressor). This optimized checkpoint lets you run Mistral Large 3 efficiently on Blackwell NVL72 systems and on a single 8×A100 or 8×H100 node using [vLLM](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm).

Markdown Content:
# Mistral supercharges voice AI with new models | The Deep View

[](https://www.thedeepview.com/)

[Sign In](https://www.thedeepview.com/auth/login)Subscribe

[Sign In](https://www.thedeepview.com/auth/login)Subscribe

[](https://www.thedeepview.com/)

[Big Tech](https://www.thedeepview.com/articles?category=big-tech)[Governance](https://www.thedeepview.com/articles?category=governance)[Hardware](https://www.thedeepview.com/articles?category=hardware)[Policy](https://www.thedeepview.com/articles?category=policy)[Products](https://www.thedeepview.com/articles?category=products)[Research](https://www.thedeepview.com/articles?category=research)[Startups](https://www.thedeepview.com/articles?category=startups)

![Image 2](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/0no9te5e/production/854341a801a2dcc9c49d9651775364d57b2123d1-1456x816.png?rect=3%2C0%2C1450%2C816&w=3840&h=2161&fit=max&auto=format)

[![Image 3: Sabrina Ortiz](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/0no9te5e/production/63ba8d4e1a0cf2df9a23424e373b06d91293a858-1080x1080.png?auto=format&fit=max&q=75&w=90) #### Sabrina Ortiz Senior Reporter](https://www.thedeepview.com/author/sabrina-ortiz)

Feb 4, 2026•3:00pm UTC

# Mistral supercharges voice AI with new models

AI assistants are going voice-first, and Mistral AI just launched its models to compete.

On Wednesday, the French AI startup launched Voxtral Transcribe 2, its next-generation family of speech-to-text models that boast state-of-the-art transcription quality, speaker diarization, and timestamps, while maintaining ultra-low latency, according to the company. The models are also small enough to run on-device, offering wins in privacy and cost.

“Voxtral Transcribe 2 proves that state-of-the-art transcription can run locally, without compromising accuracy or speed. For businesses and users who demand privacy and control, this changes everything,” said Pierre Stock, VP Science at Mistral AI, to The Deep View.

The launch includes:

*   Voxtral Realtime - A 4 billion parameter model aimed at live transcription, achieving “state of the art” transcription with 480ms latency across 13 languages. It can be configurable down to sub-200ms latency.
*   Voxtral Mini Transcribe V2 - Offers high quality transcriptions at a lower cost, with Mistral claiming it achieves “the lowest word error rate, at the lowest price point.”
*   An audio playground in Mistral Studio where users can test the transcription capabilities offered by Voxtral 2.

Performance on the FLEURS benchmark shows that Voxtral Mini Transcribe V2 performs competitively against models from Gemini and OpenAI, with the lowest diarization error rate.

https://www.ai-toolbox.co/chatgpt-models.title: ChatGPT Models Explained: Complete Comparison Guide (2026)
https://www.ai-toolbox.co/chatgpt-models.text:
URL Source: https://www.ai-toolbox.co/chatgpt-models/chatgpt-models-explained-complete-comparison-2026

Published Time: 2026-01-06T00:00:00+00:00

Markdown Content:
**ChatGPT's 2026 lineup is built around the GPT-5 family: GPT-5.3 Instant (the default for every tier, including free), GPT-5.4 Thinking (paid tiers, deeper reasoning), and GPT-5.4 Pro (the highest-capability option for Pro, Business, Enterprise, and Edu).** A "Legacy Models" tab preserves GPT-5.2 Thinking for 90 days after the GPT-5.4 launch. Older models - GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1 mini, o4-mini, and the original GPT-5 (Instant and Thinking) - were retired from ChatGPT on February 13, 2026 (OpenAI, 2026). ChatGPT now uses a single auto-switching router that picks Instant or Thinking based on the query. **ChatGPT Toolbox**, a Chrome extension with 18,000+ active users and a 4.5/5 Chrome Web Store rating, works with every current ChatGPT model and adds folders, full-text search, bulk export, prompt library, and prompt chaining to organize conversations across Instant, Thinking, and Pro modes.

OpenAI dramatically simplified the ChatGPT model picker in 2026. Where 2025 had a confusing mix of GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, GPT-4.5, o1, o3, o3-pro, and o4-mini, today there are three primary choices: **Instant**, **Thinking**, and **Pro**. Each maps to a specific capability tier in the GPT-5 family. This guide walks through every current model - capabilities, tier availability, context windows, thinking-time controls, and benchmarks - then gives you a task-first decision framework so you pick the right one every time. We'll close with real-world use cases by profession and answer the 8 questions people search most.

![Image 1: ChatGPT 2026 model lineup showing GPT-5.3 Instant, GPT-5.4 Thinking, and GPT-5.4 Pro](https://media.happyhourhub.co/ChatGPT-Toolbox-Media/blog88_1.webp)
## The 2026 ChatGPT Lineup: Only GPT-5 Family

**As of February 13, 2026, every active ChatGPT model belongs to the GPT-5 family. GPT-4 series and o-series models are retired.** OpenAI merged reasoning and general-purpose intelligence into a unified design, with one router (Instant) that can auto-switch to deeper reasoning (Thinking) when a query needs it.

The full list of retired models (Feb 13, 2026):

*   **GPT-4o** - the 2024-2025 flagship multimodal model (ChatGPT Business, Enterprise, and Edu keep GPT-4o in Custom GPTs until April 3, 2026)
*   **GPT-4.1** - the specialized coding model with a 1M token context window
*   **GPT-4.1 mini** - the lightweight version of GPT-4.1
*   **OpenAI o4-mini** - the small reasoning model in the o-series lineup
*   **GPT-5 Instant** and **GPT-5 Thinking** - the original GPT-5 models (August 2025)

API access to these retired models remains unchanged for developers with existing integrations, though OpenAI has not committed to a long-term API availability window. New projects should start on the current GPT-5.3 / GPT-5.4 models.

https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/aws-wee.title: AWS Weekly Roundup: Amazon Bedrock agent workflows, Amazon SageMaker private connectivity, and more (February 2, 2026) | Amazon Web Services
https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/aws-wee.text:
URL Source: https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/aws-weekly-roundup-amazon-bedrock-agent-workflows-amazon-sagemaker-private-connectivity-and-more-february-2-2026/

Published Time: 2026-02-02T09:19:48-08:00

Markdown Content:
## [AWS News Blog](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/)

Over the past week, we passed [Laba festival](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laba_Festival), a traditional marker in the Chinese calendar that signals the final stretch leading up to the Lunar New Year. For many in China, it’s a moment associated with reflection and preparation, wrapping up what the year has carried, and turning attention toward what lies ahead.

Looking forward, next week also brings [Lichun](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lichun), the beginning of spring and the first of the 24 solar terms. In Chinese tradition, spring is often seen as the season when growth begins and new cycles take shape. There’s a common saying that “a year’s plans begin in spring,” capturing the idea that this is a time to set one’s direction and start fresh.

**Last week’s launches**

 Here are the launches that got my attention this week:

*   [Amazon Bedrock](https://aws.amazon.com/bedrock/) enhances support for agent workflows with server-side tools and extended prompt caching – Amazon Bedrock introduced two updates that improve how developers build and operate AI agents. [The Responses API now supports server-side tool use](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/01/amazon-bedrock-server-side-custom-tools-responses-api/), so agents can perform actions such as web search, code execution, and database updates within AWS security boundaries. [Bedrock also adds a 1-hour time-to-live (TTL) option for prompt caching](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/01/amazon-bedrock-one-hour-duration-prompt-caching/), which helps improve performance and reduce the cost for long-running, multi-turn agent workflows. Server-side tools are available with OpenAI GPT OSS 20B and 120B models, and the 1-hour prompt caching TTL is generally available for select Claude models by Anthropic in Amazon Bedrock.
*   [Amazon SageMaker Unified Studio adds private VPC connectivity with AWS PrivateLink](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/01/amazon-sagemaker-unified-studio-aws-privatelink/) – [Amazon SageMaker Unified Studio](https://aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/unified-studio/) now supports AWS PrivateLink, providing private connectivity between your VPC and SageMaker Unified Studio without routing customer data over the public internet.
